case.title: Cyber Ghost
case.logline: A hacker who exposed city council corruption is found dead ten days after the leak — and the evidence suggests the whistleblower was silenced from the inside.

setting.atmosphere: A mid-sized city where civic trust is already fractured. Rain-slicked streets, fluorescent government offices, and a co-working space that smells like cold brew and paranoia.

victim.name: Jordan Mercer
victim.occupation: Freelance web developer / anonymous hacktivist operating as 'Cyber Ghost'
victim.cause_of_death: Fatal fentanyl overdose — initially ruled accidental, but toxicology and scene anomalies suggest staging

suspects.name: Aldric Voss
suspects.occupation: City Council political consultant and fixer
suspects.why_suspicious: Publicly has no direct motive — his role is purely advisory. But financial records show his consulting fees spiked 340% in the same fiscal year as the Briarfield contracts.

suspects.name: Nadia Osei
suspects.why_suspicious: Jordan allegedly stole her exploit and burned her operational security — she had publicly threatened Cyber Ghost on forums two weeks before the death.

suspects.name: Serena Cho
suspects.occupation: City IT and finance department systems administrator
suspects.why_suspicious: She provided the credentials — if Jordan is identified, she faces federal charges for unauthorized access and obstruction. She had every reason to ensure Jordan was silenced before a full investigation could trace the leak back to her.

emotional_arc.envelope_A: Curiosity and civic unease — a whistleblower is dead and the documents they died for are still online. The player feels the weight of a cover-up already in motion, and every document feels like it could be tampered.
emotional_arc.envelope_B: Suspicion fracturing in three directions — Nadia's threats, Serena's cryptic warning, and Voss's suspiciously clean public image all pull the player toward different conclusions. Each interview generates empathy then doubt.
emotional_arc.envelope_C: Cold shock and vindication — the router log places an invisible visitor in a dead man's apartment. The pivot from 'overdose' to 'premeditated murder by a political fixer' reframes every document the player has already read.
emotional_arc.envelope_R: Exhausted satisfaction and moral weight — justice feels bureaucratic and incomplete, but the evidence chain is airtight. The player feels the specific tragedy of a whistleblower killed not for being wrong, but for being right.

scenes_for_video.location: Apartment 4C, 812 Kellner Street, Harwick — Jordan Mercer's apartment
scenes_for_video.visual_description: A compact one-bedroom apartment on the fourth floor of a mid-rise residential building in the Civic District. The desk in the northeast corner holds a laptop in sleep mode, its indicator light pulsing slowly. The pill organizer sits open on the kitchen counter near the sink. Blinds partially drawn. Nothing overturned. The apartment has the stillness of a space that has been arranged rather than lived in that morning.
scenes_for_video.lighting: Dim — morning light filtering through half-closed venetian blinds, a single LED strip under kitchen cabinets left on
scenes_for_video.atmosphere: Uncanny quiet. The kind of stillness that building superintendent Remy Salazar felt before he knocked. Civic District street noise muted four floors up. No music, no movement.
notable_objects: laptop in sleep mode on desk
open pill organizer on counter (Tuesday slot)
glass near sink
no signs of forced entry or struggle
deadbolt locked from inside

scenes_for_video.location: HPD Detective Bureau — case file assembly room
scenes_for_video.visual_description: A standard HPD detective bureau workspace with fluorescent overhead lighting and a large corkboard. Four POI folders are laid out on a metal table. Each folder contains a printed photo, bio summary, and flagged financial or behavioral notes. The Voss folder has a yellow sticky on the outside reading 'consulting fees — FLAG.'
scenes_for_video.lighting: Fluorescent overhead, harsh and even, no natural light
scenes_for_video.atmosphere: Procedural and methodical. Det. Holt's workspace is organized but under pressure — a fresh homicide with a civic corruption angle and city hall eyes already on it.
notable_objects: four POI folders on metal table
corkboard with case number header
yellow sticky on Voss folder
victim device metadata printout

scenes_for_video.location: Harwick City Hall — IT Security Division office, Floor 2
scenes_for_video.visual_description: A windowless server-adjacent office in City Hall's IT security wing. Rows of workstations with dual monitors, most showing log dashboards. The print queue audit log is open on workstation CHWS-0009. A second monitor on an adjacent station shows the same file with different session metadata — accessed from Suite 440, Floor 4, the council fixer's office suite, without annotation.
scenes_for_video.lighting: Fluorescent ceiling panels, no natural light, monitor glow on workstation surfaces
scenes_for_video.atmosphere: The invisible kind of intrusion. No broken doors. No alarms. Just a cursor that moved through a system it had no business touching, 18 minutes and 42 seconds, then gone.
notable_objects: print queue access log open on CHWS-0009
unregistered guest session from Suite 440 logged at 16:45:03
memo signed by T. Bannister dated October 13
no annotation against the Suite 440 entry
scenes_for_video.time_of_day: Daytime — original audit October 13, unauthorized access October 14 at 16:45

scenes_for_video.location: HPD Digital Crimes Unit — Harwick Police Headquarters, analyst workstation
scenes_for_video.visual_description: A dark-lit analyst bay with multiple monitors showing Tor mirror site captures, VPN trace maps, and PGP key verification outputs. Analyst Quiñones has printed a side-by-side comparison of the first and second Briarfield document caches, with yellow highlighting on the sections that were edited out and red annotations where new attribution markers were inserted pointing toward a Finance Division employee.
scenes_for_video.lighting: Low ambient, blue-tinted monitor glow dominant, single desk lamp
scenes_for_video.atmosphere: The digital trace of a dead person's identity being used against them. Quiñones works carefully, noting what can be proven and what cannot — the misdirection is elegant, and that's the most disturbing part.
notable_objects: side-by-side cache comparison printout with yellow and red markings
VPN trace map showing HWK-VPN-Exit-07
PGP key fingerprint verification printout
timestamp log showing 02:17:44 publication event

scenes_for_video.location: Multiple — digital platforms compiled as evidence package
scenes_for_video.visual_description: Four platform captures laid out as an HPD evidence compilation. The X/Twitter thread shows journalist Priya Nair's live-coverage from October 12, a council staffer's confirming reply visible below. The Reddit r/Harwick thread shows an upvoted post with a buried throwaway comment. Nadia Osei's Instagram post shows her at a hackathon whiteboard, upload timestamp 00:52 visible. The Facebook entry shows the Harwick Civic Watch post with Remy Salazar's haunted comment and a screenshot-within-screenshot of the since-deleted reply from HarwickProspers2024.
scenes_for_video.lighting: Screen captures — each platform's native UI with its own color temperature (X/Twitter dark mode gray-black, Reddit orange-white, Instagram gradient white, Facebook light blue-white)
scenes_for_video.atmosphere: The internet processing a death it doesn't know is a murder yet. Public grief, conspiracy theories, and one comment that got deleted too fast mixing together in a feed no one fully controls.
notable_objects: Priya Nair X/Twitter thread from October 12
Reddit throwaway comment about 847-page print session
Nadia Instagram post timestamped 00:52 Oct 20
deleted HarwickProspers2024 Facebook comment preserved as screenshot

social_media_plan_from_case.case_purpose: A local journalist live-threads the Briarfield leak story — plants the timeline of the original publication, introduces Cyber Ghost as a persona, and includes a reply from a suspected city council staffer that looks like damage control but actually confirms a key detail about the Briarfield vendor list.

A post in r/Harwick titled 'Did the city just kill Cyber Ghost?' — citizen speculation that mixes genuine deductive reasoning with wild conspiracy theories. One comment from a throwaway account accurately describes the print queue as the leak's origin vector, which the player must evaluate as either a genuine insider leak or a red herring.

Nadia Osei's personal account — a post timestamped during the hackathon showing her at a whiteboard, establishing her presence at Meridian Co-Works. Comments include a timestamp from a tagged attendee that corroborates part of her alibi, but the post itself was uploaded at 12:52am — after she had badged back in.

A community post from the Harwick Civic Watch group reacting to the death — includes a comment from Remy Salazar (the building super who found Jordan) that adds texture to the scene discovery, and a reply from an account linked to Voss's political network that was deleted within 6 hours but is preserved in a screenshot.

hook_angles_from_case: A hacker who exposed city council corruption is found dead ten days after the leak — and the evidence suggests the whistleblower was silenced from the inside.
Jordan Mercer — Fatal fentanyl overdose — initially ruled accidental, but toxicology and scene anomalies suggest staging
